question.text: How often must Category I and II AIFs undertake valuation of their investments?

answer.text: At least once in every 6 months by an independent valuer
At least once every quarter by an independent valuer
At least once a year by an independent valuer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Category I & II AIF shall undertake valuation of investments at least once in every 6 months by an independent valuer. The period may be enhanced to one year with approval of at least 75% of investors by value. **The Trap:** Annual valuation requires 75% investor approval; the default is semi-annual. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The independent valuer cannot be affiliated with the manager, sponsor, or trustee of the AIF.
